attacking_style: Favors long-range solo attacks from 8-12km out on final climbs, often from breakaways rather than GC group. Shows willingness to attack companions in breaks to maximize stage win chances over GC position.
team_tactics: Often operates with minimal team support; Israel had him work for Woods' KOM in 2021 Tour, Jayco prioritizes stage wins over his GC. Functions best as opportunistic breakaway rider rather than protected GC leader.
terrain_preference: Excels on single long sustained climbs where diesel climbing dominates. Struggles on repeated HC climbs and explosive short accelerations. Confident descender (held 12km solo attack including downhill sections).
vulnerability: Cannot respond to explosive GC accelerations in final 3km; poor sprint finish from reduced groups costs GC seconds. Collapses spectacularly on multi-climb HC days (lost 9+ minutes Tour 2022, 3m56s Tour 2021).
key_patterns: Long-range solo attacks 8-12km from finish, particularly from breakaways (Catalunya, Giro 2020, Tour 2021)
Vulnerable to explosive accelerations under 3km: dropped when Almeida/Quintana went 2.7km, surprised by attacks
Major GC collapses on repeated HC climb stages: lost 9+ min Tour 2022, 3m56s Tour 2021
Sprint weakness costs seconds in reduced group finishes despite climbing strength (Romandie, Dauphine)
Parasitic GC tactics: maximizes position without leading, exploits team confusion for personal gain
Strong back-to-back mountain stage recovery: attacked 8km day after second place Giro 2020
form_trajectory: positive: returned to 2021 Tour form in 2023 with repeated late attacks at Tour and podium position at Dauphine, suggesting GC consistency restored after 2022 collapse
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 15
